generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance BuildingControlEngineer : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Professional responsible for modeling and configuring building devices, defining control logic within BIM environments for operational control"
]

instance FacilityManager : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Operational role that remotely interacts with physical infrastructure via Digital Twin interfaces for monitoring and control"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor integrated into BIM models for capturing real-time temperature data across building infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor integrated into BIM models for capturing real-time humidity data across building infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor detecting occupancy patterns integrated into BIM models for spatial context awareness in building operations"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Generic environmental sensor capturing parameters like air quality, pressure, or noise levels within building infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance HumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically rich, object-based 3D model capturing geometric and spatial characteristics of building components generated via BIM"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoDataIngestion, UnrealEngineVisualization
    base:isContainedIn AECOProjectEnvironment
]

instance BIMFunctionalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM model enriched with functional attributes and performance dependencies between building components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoStateManagement, ModelDrivenTransformationPipeline
    base:isContainedIn AECOProjectEnvironment
]

instance EnhancedBIMBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavioral model extending BIM with control logic and operational states for runtime operations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoStateManagement, CustomServiceIntegration
    base:isContainedIn AECOProjectEnvironment
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously synchronized data stream representing current state of physical building infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, HumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoStateManagement, UnrealEngineVisualization
]

instance ControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Configuration data defining building control logic and operational constraints for automated systems"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ControlLogicDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseDittoStateManagement
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoDataIngestion : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Secure, scalable IoT data ingestion layer for standardized protocol support (MQTT, CoAP) enabling bidirectional data exchange"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataSynchronizationService, BidirectionalControlService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinFramework
]

instance EclipseDittoStateManagement : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin state management platform providing real-time synchronization via event-driven architecture and CRUD operations"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataSynchronizationService, ControlLogicValidationService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinFramework
]

instance ModelDrivenTransformationPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated model-to-model transformation framework converting enriched BIM models into deployable Digital Twin artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelDrivenDeploymentService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinFramework
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-performance rendering engine for immersive 3D visualization supporting real-time updates and physics simulations"
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService, PerformanceSimulationService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinFramework
]

instance CustomServiceIntegration : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular architecture extension points enabling plug-and-play integration of domain-specific services and analytics"
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomAnalyticsService, DomainSpecificExtensionService
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinFramework
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling live synchronization between physical assets and digital twins with sub-second latency for operational data"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight, ClosedLoopControlAction
]

instance ModelDrivenDeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated deployment process reducing manual intervention by ≥90% for scalable Digital Twin configurations"
    DTDFVocab:provides ReproducibleConfigurationInsight, StreamlinedExecutionAction
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization service enabling users to interactively monitor and navigate digital twins in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:provides ScenarioTestingInsight, VirtualWalkthroughAction
]

instance ControlLogicValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service allowing Building Control Engineers to validate and adjust control logic based on Digital Twin feedback"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlValidationInsight, LogicAdjustmentAction
]

instance PerformanceSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting energy consumption, structural integrity, and occupancy scenario simulations with dynamic updates"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyAnalysisInsight, StructuralHealthInsight
]

instance BidirectionalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling closed-loop control between physical infrastructure and Digital Twin for operational optimization"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalOptimizationInsight, RemoteControlAction
]

instance CustomAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling custom analytics and data processing for domain-specific requirements"
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomAnalyticsInsight
]

instance DomainSpecificExtensionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing domain-specific extensions and custom functionality"
    DTDFVocab:provides DomainSpecificInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization of system states and operational metrics for Facility Managers in real-time"
]

instance ClosedLoopControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control adjustments based on real-time data synchronization between physical and digital representations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ReproducibleConfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Verification of standardized, reproducible Digital Twin configurations through model-driven deployment"
]

instance StreamlinedExecutionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated execution of Digital Twin deployment processes with minimal manual intervention"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ScenarioTestingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation results from testing various occupancy and operational scenarios within the Digital Twin environment"
]

instance VirtualWalkthroughAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "User-initiated navigation through immersive 3D representations of building infrastructure"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results of building control logic against real-time operational data from the Digital Twin"
]

instance LogicAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Engineer-initiated adjustments to control logic parameters based on validation insights"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EnergyAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Performance analysis of energy consumption patterns derived from real-time and historical data"
]

instance StructuralHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural integrity assessment results from simulation and real-time monitoring data"
]

instance OperationalOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Recommendations for optimizing building operations based on real-time performance metrics"
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Facility Manager-initiated remote control commands executed through the Digital Twin interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance CustomAnalyticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Results from custom analytics processing applied to Digital Twin data"
]

instance DomainSpecificInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Domain-specific insights derived from specialized analysis of Digital Twin data"
]

// C12: Environments/Components
instance AECOProjectEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Collaborative platform supporting multi-disciplinary coordination across Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations"
    base:contains DigitalTwinFramework, BuildingInfrastructureComponent
]

instance BuildingInfrastructureComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical building infrastructure including structural elements, MEP systems, and façade assemblies with integrated sensors"
]

instance DigitalTwinFramework : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Centralized Digital Twin hub coordinating bidirectional data exchange and service integration"
]

instance ControlLogicDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ControlLogicModel
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework enables **bidirectional control flows** from the virtual model to physical assets via a centralized hub that feeds control signals back to Building Control Systems (BCS). While the document highlights the framework’s ability to **synchronize operational commands** and **adjust runtime configurations**, specific details such as command types (e.g., JSON, OPC UA formats), validation/authentication mechanisms (e.g., RBAC, certificate-based), or conflict resolution strategies are not provided. The system emphasizes **adaptability** through runtime configuration management, allowing adjustments without full redeployment, but lacks explicit technical specifications for security protocols, feedback systems, or command execution confirmation processes."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical building assets and their digital counterparts, with an implicit requirement for **low-latency bidirectional communication** (though explicit millisecond-to-second-scale or sub-second granularity specifications are not provided). The framework supports **live synchronization** through runtime configuration management, enabling dynamic adjustments without full redeployment, though precise temporal granularity (e.g., millisecond, second, or minute-level) is not detailed in the documents. The system emphasizes **operational responsiveness** for Facility Managers and Building Control Engineers, aligning with dynamic operational needs in the AECO domain rather than static BIM representations. Latency requirements are implied to be sufficient for real-time monitoring and control but are not quantified beyond the term 'real-time.'"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **centralized orchestration pattern** with a **hub-and-spoke architecture**, where the DT Framework acts as the central hub facilitating bidirectional data flow between enriched BIM models and operational twins. Key components include:
  - **Enriched BIM models** as the foundational data source,
  - **Custom services** integrated via modular extension points,
  - **Runtime configuration management** for dynamic adjustments,
  - **Role-based interaction layers** (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers).
  Resource management is implied through **scalability requirements** (from components to entire infrastructure) and **low-overhead updates** for maintainability. System-wide coordination relies on **standardized interfaces** and **modular service integration**, enabling evolution without full redeployment. The framework does not explicitly detail distributed orchestration (e.g., federated twins) or specific coordination protocols (e.g., Kafka, MQTT), but the emphasis on **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** suggests adherence to **industry standards** (e.g., OPC UA, BACnet) for component integration."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in this framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **runtime operation and evolution**, addressing the gap between BIM’s static representations and DTs’ dynamic nature. Key phases include:

    1. **Device Modeling & Configuration**:
       - **Representation**: BIM models are enriched with Building Control System (BCS) components (sensors, actuators, controllers) by Building Control Engineers.
       - **Transition**: Manual configuration of control logic and device definitions within BIM, bridging static BIM to dynamic DT requirements.
       - **Governance**: Governed by functional requirements (e.g., F_X in Table III) to ensure semantic consistency for subsequent transformations.

    2. **Model-Driven Transformation**:
       - **Representation**: Enriched BIM models are transformed into structured, deployable DT artifacts via Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles.
       - **Transition**: Automated extraction of semantic representations from BIM, minimizing manual intervention.
       - **Governance**: Aligned with theoretical and practical insights from projects like TwinLight and BOREALIS, ensuring alignment with operational needs.

    3. **Deployment & Runtime Operation**:
       - **Representation**: Deployed DTs with real-time synchronization between physical infrastructure and digital twins, supported by interactive visualization.
       - **Transition**: Continuous synchronization ensures dynamic updates, while modular architecture allows runtime extension.
       - **Governance**: Facilitated by standardized extension points for custom services, enabling adaptability to evolving operational requirements.

    4. **Customization & Evolution**:
       - **Representation**: DTs evolve through integration of custom services/plugins by DT Engineers, extending functionality.
       - **Transition**: Modular architecture supports seamless integration of new services, ensuring scalability.
       - **Governance**: Governed by modular design principles and extension points, enabling iterative improvements without disrupting core operations.

    **Stakeholder Roles**:
    - Building Control Engineers drive initial modeling and control logic definition.
    - Facility Managers monitor and operate via the DT during runtime.
    - DT Engineers extend functionality through custom services.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The framework addresses **fidelity challenges** by bridging the gap between **BIM’s static representations** and the **dynamic operational needs** of Digital Twins. **Semantic drift**—where alignment between digital models and physical counterparts diverges over time—is acknowledged as a critical consideration. While the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement metric** (mentioned in the abstract) is implied as a validation method, explicit **validation and verification (V&V) procedures**, **uncertainty quantification**, or **error detection/correction mechanisms** are not detailed in the provided documents. Quality assurance processes are inferred to rely on the framework’s **model-driven automation** and **real-time synchronization** but lack specific technical implementations."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework integrates **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** to ensure **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, supporting real-time data exchange and bidirectional communication. The framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** enable seamless integration with diverse protocols, though specific communication protocols (e.g., HTTP/REST, OPC UA), network topology (e.g., star-topology mesh), or bandwidth/latency requirements (e.g., 10 Mbps) are not documented. The document emphasizes **scalability** and **reliability** through runtime configuration management and low-overhead updates, but no details on redundancy mechanisms, security protocols (e.g., TLS 1.3, VLANs), or latency targets are provided."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework is deployed within a **modular architecture** that supports **standardized extension points**, enabling custom services and analytics integration. While the document mentions **Unreal Engine** for immersive visualization and a **runtime configuration management** system for adaptability, no specific details are provided regarding hosting platforms (e.g., cloud/edge/on-premise), computational/storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, or deployment models (e.g., Kubernetes, Ansible). The framework’s adaptability is implied through low-overhead updates and interoperability, but hardware specifications (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson, Intel Xeon), orchestration tools, or availability/reliability assurances are not documented."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through **standardized extension points** and **modular integration patterns**, addressing NF_X requirements for interoperability. While specific protocols like **OPC UA** or **BACnet** are not mentioned, the framework leverages **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for data exchange and integration with external systems. The system integrates with **BIM tools** (e.g., Revit-like environments) for initial device modeling and deployment, though the exact BIM tooling is not specified beyond 'BIM tools.' Horizontal integration is facilitated by:
  - **Bidirectional data exchange** with external systems via the central hub,
  - **Modular services** that can be extended or replaced without disrupting the core framework,
  - **Non-functional requirements (NF_X)** ensuring compatibility with enterprise systems and other Digital Twins, though explicit integration patterns (e.g., API gateways, middleware) are not detailed.
  The system does not explicitly outline dependencies on external systems beyond the implied use of **BIM tools** and **Eclipse-based middleware** for connectivity."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The framework adheres to **BIM (Building Information Modeling) standards** as the foundational representation layer for DT deployment, addressing BIM’s limitations in dynamic system modeling. **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles govern the transformation of BIM models into deployable DT artifacts, ensuring structured and automated workflows. Interoperability is facilitated by **standardized extension points** within the modular architecture, enabling seamless integration of custom services and third-party tools.

No explicit mention of **industry standards (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010, IEC 62424, or OPC UA)** or **certification requirements** is present in the documents. Compliance frameworks are not detailed beyond the **functional requirements (F_X)**, which implicitly govern DT lifecycle governance. The framework does not reference **interoperability standards** beyond the implied use of BIM and MDE for cross-domain compatibility.
"
]
